title: Ensure that the Expiration Date is set for all Secrets in  RBAC Key Vaults

input_row.title: Ensure that the Expiration Date is set for all Secrets in  RBAC Key Vaults
input_row.description: Ensure that all Secrets in Role Based Access Control (RBAC) Azure Key Vaults have an expiration date set.
input_row.rationale: The Azure Key Vault enables users to store and keep secrets within the Microsoft Azure environment. Secrets in the Azure Key Vault are octet sequences with a maximum size of 25k bytes each. The exp (expiration date) attribute identifies the expiration date on or after which the secret MUST NOT be used. By default, secrets never expire. It is thus recommended to rotate secrets in the key vault and set an explicit expiration date for all secrets. This ensures that the secrets cannot be used beyond their assigned lifetimes.
input_row.audit: Audit from Azure Portal 1. Go to Key vaults. 2. For each Key vault, click on Secrets. 3. In the main pane, ensure that the status of the secret is Enabled. 4. For each enabled secret, ensure that an appropriate Expiration date is set. Audit from Azure CLI Ensure that the output of the below command contains ID (id), enabled status as true and Expiration date (expires) is not empty or null: az keyvault secret list --vault-name --query '[*].{"kid":kid,"enabled":attributes.enabled,"expires":attributes.expires}' Audit from PowerShell Retrieve a list of Key vaults: Get-AzKeyVault For each Key vault, run the following command to determine which vaults are configured to use RBAC: Get-AzKeyVault -VaultName For each Key vault with the EnableRbacAuthorization setting set to True, run the following command: Get-AzKeyVaultSecret -VaultName Make sure the Expires setting is configured with a value as appropriate wherever the Enabled setting is set to True. Audit from Azure Policy If referencing a digital copy of this Benchmark, clicking a Policy ID will open a link to the associated Policy definition in Azure. If referencing a printed copy, you can search Policy IDs from this URL: https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_Azure_Policy/PolicyMenuBlade/~/Definitions • Policy ID: 98728c90-32c7-4049-8429-847dc0f4fe37 - Name: 'Key Vault secrets should have an expiration date'
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure Portal 1. Go to Key vaults. 2. For each Key vault, click on Secrets. 3. In the main pane, ensure that the status of the secret is Enabled. 4. For each enabled secret, ensure that an appropriate Expiration date is set. Remediate from Azure CLI Update the Expiration date for the secret using the below command: az keyvault secret set-attributes --name --vault-name --expires Y-m-d'T'H:M:S'Z' Note: To view the expiration date on all secrets in a Key Vault using Microsoft API, the List Secret permission is required. To update the expiration date for the secrets: 1. Go to the Key vault, click on Access Control (IAM). 2. Click on Add role assignment and assign the role of Key Vault Secrets Officer to the appropriate user. Remediate from PowerShell Set-AzKeyVaultSecretAttribute -VaultName -Name - Expires
input_row.impact: Secrets cannot be used beyond their assigned expiry date respectively. Secrets need to be rotated periodically wherever they are used.
input_row.default_value: By default, secrets do not expire.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_kms_secret_expiration_date_set

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **AWS API Calls / Boto3 SDK Methods:**
  - Use `boto3.client('kms')` to interact with AWS Key Management Service for managing encryption keys and secrets.
  - Specifically, use `list_aliases()` to retrieve the aliases for KMS keys which can also indicate if they are tied to any secrets.
  
- **AWS CLI Commands:**
  - Use `aws kms list-aliases` to list aliases and ensure secrets are associated with valid KMS keys.
  
- **Resources to Enumerate:**
  - Enumerate all KMS key aliases, then check the associated secrets in AWS Secrets Manager. If Key Vault-like behavior is implemented in Secrets Manager, verify each secret set.
  
- **Configuration Properties/Settings to Validate:**
  - Check for the expiration property of secrets in Secrets Manager. Validation should confirm if expiration dates have been set.
  
- **Pass/Fail Criteria with Expected Values:**
  - A secret is compliant if it has the `ExpirationDate` attribute set and is not `None` or empty.
  
- **Example Checks:**
  - Use `boto3.client('secretsmanager').list_secrets()` to list all secrets and then iterate through each secret to check `ExpirationDate`.
  - A pass would be defined as each secret returning an `ExpirationDate` value that is a valid date and not empty, ensuring all secrets are configured correctly for expiration.

This approach ensures that we can automate the audit of the expiration dates for the secrets stored in AWS platforms akin to the Azure Key Vault, enabling a strong security posture regarding sensitive information.
